title: Psychotria gabrieliae
intro: Psychotria gabrieliae, also known as Gabriel's tree or Gabriel vine, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The health benefits of Psychotria gabrieliae include its use in traditional medicine to treat various conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Psychotria gabrieliae include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support immune function and relieve symptoms of respiratory and skin infections.
taxonomy: Psychotria gabrieliae is classified within the Rubiaceae family, which is a large family of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This family includes approximately 600 genera and over 13,000 species. Psychotria gabrieliae is part of the Psychotria genus, which is a group of flowering plants native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. The Psychotria genus comprises over 1,800 species, many of which are used in traditional medicine or as ornamental plants. Psychotria gabrieliae is native to the tropical forests of New Guinea, where it can be found growing as an epiphyte or terrestrial plant.
